title: malus × oxysepala
intro: Malus × oxysepala, a hybrid apple species, is a rare and lesser-known plant that combines the characteristics of Malus baccata and Malus sieversii. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus × oxysepala include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, Malus × oxysepala is often used in the form of teas, tinctures, and extracts, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
